associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct a `Camera` via the native constructor.

associated_functions.name: try_get_matrix
associated_functions.doc:
Cpp analog of rust's `GameObjectExt::get_matrix`. Reach is the same
 (every derived type has an implicit `operator GameObject&()` so a
 derived `obj.try_get_matrix(&m)` works without an explicit cast).
 Returns `bool` rather than `std::optional<Matrix4>` to keep the
 `<optional>` include out of the public surface.

variants.name: Max
variants.doc: The actiion mapper only supports 256 actions.

fields.name: mem_size_and_flags
fields.doc:
Packed allocation size + flags. Low 15 bits are the chunk size
 in bytes (as allocated from the Havok thread allocator); the
 high bit is a flag.

associated_functions.name: refcount
associated_functions.doc:
Named `refcount` rather than `reference_count` to avoid colliding
 with the field of the same name.

associated_functions.name: allocation_size
associated_functions.doc: Packed allocation size: low 15 bits of `mem_size_and_flags`.

associated_functions.name: reference_count
associated_functions.doc:
Current reference count. Named `reference_count` in Rust (no
 field/method collision); C++ uses `refcount` to avoid the clash.

associated_functions.name: pyxis_hk_as_referenced_object
associated_functions.doc:
Returns a pointer to the embedded `HkReferencedObject` field.
 The `HkRefCounted<T>` template uses this to drive ref/unref via
 `referenced_object.hpp`'s free helpers; each shape type exposes
 it because the field path differs per type (direct vs nested
 inside `HkpShape`).

associated_functions.name: pyxis_hk_free
associated_functions.doc:
Tear-down hook invoked from `HkRefCounted<T>::release()` once the
 reference count hits zero. Calls the `#[address]`-bound destructor
 with `free=true` so the Havok thread-memory chunk gets reclaimed.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct an `HkpRigidBodyCInfo` via the native constructor.

associated_functions.name: make_heap
associated_functions.doc:
Heap-allocates a `PfxRigidBody` and runs the native ctor against
 the provided `HkpRigidBody`. Caller takes ownership; tear down
 via `destructor(false)` + `delete` or wrap in a smart-pointer.
 Mirrors rust's `unsafe fn new() -> Pin<Box<Self>>`.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct an `AIRayCastFilter` via the native constructor.

associated_functions.name: make
associated_functions.doc:
Default-constructed `RayCastResult`: forward-pointing normal,
 `distance = 1`, sentinel `0xFFFFFFFF` in every `unknown` slot.
 Matches the shape rust's `Default` impl produces.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc:
Default-constructed `RayCastResult`: forward-pointing normal,
 `distance = 1`, sentinel `0xFFFFFFFF` in every `unknown` slot.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct a `GenericRenderBlock` via the native constructor.

kind.doc:
`ManuallyDrop<SharedPtr<u32>>` is used instead of `SharedPtr<u32>` to avoid
 the `Drop` implementation of `SharedPtr<u32>` being called when the `RenderBlock`
 is dropped. The destructor, which we call in `drop`, will decrement the refcount
 for us.

associated_functions.name: make
associated_functions.doc:
JC2 ships no `constructor()` symbol for this type; the factory
 builds one by zeroing the struct then assigning the known-good
 vftable pointer, sentinel text/font hashes, and default text-
 render flags (`render_style = 2`, `font_size = 16`).

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc:
Construct a `RenderBlock3DText` by zeroing the struct and
 assigning the known-good vftable, sentinel hashes, and defaults.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct a `RenderBlockGeneral` via the native constructor.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct a `RenderBlockLine` via the native constructor.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Construct a `RenderBlockTriangle` via the native constructor.

associated_functions.name: make
associated_functions.doc:
JC2 ships no `constructor()` symbol for this type; the factory
 returns a zeroed instance with `colors[0]` and `colors[1]` primed
 to opaque white. The remaining fields are filled in by
 `initialize_matrix` / `update_matrix`.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc:
Construct a `RenderBlockInstanceInfo` with `colors[0]` and
 `colors[1]` primed to opaque white; remaining fields are zeroed.

associated_functions.name: as_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Raw pointer to the row-major data, for passing to engine APIs.

associated_functions.name: as_mut_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Mutable raw pointer to the row-major data.

associated_functions.name: as_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Raw pointer to the row-major data, for passing to engine APIs.

associated_functions.name: as_mut_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Mutable raw pointer to the row-major data.

associated_functions.name: clone_
associated_functions.doc:
Bumps `count_impl`'s use_count and returns a fresh `SharedCount`
 over the same refcount block. Cpp-only because the rust side
 gets the same effect via `Clone`.

associated_functions.name: release
associated_functions.doc:
Decrement the strong count; on reaching zero, call `dispose`
 then `weak_release`.

associated_functions.name: weak_release
associated_functions.doc: Decrement the weak count; on reaching zero, call `destroy`.

associated_functions.name: add_ref_copy
associated_functions.doc: Increment the strong count.

associated_functions.name: weak_add_ref
associated_functions.doc: Increment the weak count.

associated_functions.name: exists
associated_functions.doc: `true` if the stored pointer is non-null.

associated_functions.name: refcount
associated_functions.doc: Current strong reference count, or 0 if the pointer is null.

associated_functions.name: is_only_owner
associated_functions.doc: `true` when this is the sole strong owner (refcount == 1).

associated_functions.name: static_cast_to
associated_functions.doc:
Bit-cast to `SharedPtr<Y>` while preserving the refcount block.
 Caller asserts that T can be reinterpreted as Y.

 SHARE semantics: bumps the refcount; the original `SharedPtr<T>`
 stays valid and the returned `SharedPtr<Y>` co-owns the same
 refcount block. Mirrors `std::static_pointer_cast<Y>`.

associated_functions.name: static_pointer_cast
associated_functions.doc:
Bit-cast to `SharedPtr<Y>` while preserving the refcount block.
 Caller asserts that T can be reinterpreted as Y.

 MOVE semantics: the Rust implementation consumes `self`. The
 original `SharedPtr<T>` is invalidated; the returned `SharedPtr<Y>`
 inherits its refcount block with no atomic increment. If you need
 to keep the `T` view alongside the `Y` view, call `.clone()` first.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc: Constructs a null `SharedPtr<T>` (refcount block is null).

associated_functions.name: from_shared
associated_functions.doc:
Static factory: bumps `shared`'s weak_count and wraps its
 count_impl in a `WeakCount`. No-self method - mirrors the rust
 `WeakCount::new_from_shared` associated fn shape.

associated_functions.name: from_shared
associated_functions.doc:
Static factory: makes a `WeakPtr<T>` borrowing the refcount
 block of an existing `SharedPtr<T>`. Bumps the weak_count.

associated_functions.name: ctor
associated_functions.doc: Default-construct via MSVCP80's `std::basic_string::basic_string()`.

associated_functions.name: ctor_cstr
associated_functions.doc:
Construct from a NUL-terminated C string via MSVCP80's
 `std::basic_string::basic_string(const char*)`.

associated_functions.name: dtor
associated_functions.doc: Destructor; does not free `self`'s storage.

associated_functions.name: copy
associated_functions.doc: Copy-assign from `other` via MSVCP80's `operator=`.

associated_functions.name: new
associated_functions.doc:
Default-construct an empty `StdString` via MSVCP80's
 `std::basic_string::basic_string()`.

associated_functions.name: is_vehicle_loaded
associated_functions.doc:
Returns true once any entry in `vehicle_types.loaded_models`
 matches `id` and is in load state 2 (loaded). Walks up to
 `vehicle_types.hash_edacd65b_likely_max_models` entries.

associated_functions.name: is_vehicle_loaded
associated_functions.doc:
Returns true once any entry in `vehicle_types.loaded_models`
 matches `id` and is in load state 2 (loaded). Walks up to
 `vehicle_types.hash_edacd65b_likely_max_models` entries.

::physics::havok::referenced_object::HkRefCounted<HkpSphereShape> HkpSphereShape_make_refcounted(float radius);
::physics::havok::referenced_object::HkRefCounted<HkpCapsule> HkpCapsule_make_refcounted(const ::physics::havok::math::HkVector4* start, const ::physics::havok::math::HkVector4* end, float radius);

::physics::havok::referenced_object::HkRefCounted<HkpCylinderShape> HkpCylinderShape_make_refcounted(const ::physics::havok::math::HkVector4* start, const ::physics::havok::math::HkVector4* end, float radius, float padding);

inline const float* Matrix3::as_ptr() const { return data; }
inline float* Matrix3::as_mut_ptr() { return data; }

inline const float* Matrix4::as_ptr() const { return data; }
inline float* Matrix4::as_mut_ptr() { return data; }
